forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.key_change: Jensen Huang claims AGI achieved but narrow definition; Microsoft CEO disagrees; no consensus shift
forecasts.base_rate: No historical precedent for AGI; expert median estimates cluster 2028-2040

factors_up.factor: Jensen Huang publicly claims AGI achieved on Lex Fridman podcast (Mar 30)
Rapid capability gains in agentic AI and software engineering tasks
Shane Legg (DeepMind cofounder): 50% chance minimal AGI by 2028

factors_down.factor: Microsoft CEO Nadella says AGI as defined won't be achieved anytime soon
Stanford shift from evangelism to evaluation suggests maturation not breakthrough
No agreed definition of AGI; goalpost-moving undermines claims

forecasts.key_uncertainties: Definition of AGI remains contested; current systems excel at narrow tasks but lack generality; 3.75 years remain
forecasts.update_triggers: Credible lab announcement of AGI; major benchmark breakthrough on novel reasoning; definitive expert consensus shift

forecasts.question: Will economic consultancy lose ≥33% value/jobs by end 2030 due to AI?
forecasts.key_change: UK consulting industry projected to grow 5.7% in 2026; AI driving demand rather than destroying it
forecasts.base_rate: No major professional services industry has lost 33%+ value in 5 years from technology disruption

factors_up.factor: 45% of consultant activities could be automated with existing technology
Firms shifting from pyramid to diamond structure; fewer junior hires
Mid-tier firms face existential squeeze between global and boutique

factors_down.factor: AI is biggest driver of consulting demand; firms growing revenue from AI services
UK consulting projected to grow 5.7% in 2026 and 7.4% in 2027
New high-value roles emerging: AI Strategy, AI Ethics, AI Governance

forecasts.key_uncertainties: Whether AI replaces consultants or creates new consulting demand; speed of client adoption; regulatory requirements for human oversight
forecasts.update_triggers: Major consulting firm layoffs attributed to AI; sustained revenue decline in Big 4; client surveys showing AI replacing consultant engagements

forecasts.question: Will Labour be largest party at next UK General Election?
forecasts.key_change: Polls stable with Reform leading; Labour at 18% vs Reform 26%; MRP projects catastrophic Labour seat losses
forecasts.base_rate: Governing parties in UK have recovered from mid-term polling deficits before but Labour's deficit is historically severe

factors_up.factor: 3+ years until election deadline (Aug 2029); significant time for recovery
Reform may struggle to maintain support as they face greater scrutiny
FPTP system could still favor Labour in some seat configurations

factors_down.factor: MRP projects Labour falling to 85 seats (from 411); Reform at 381 seats
Labour at 18% in polls, behind Reform (26%) and level with Conservatives (18%)
Approval ratings approaching historic lows for a governing party

forecasts.key_uncertainties: Whether Reform can sustain support and convert it to seats under FPTP; economic conditions by 2029; potential leadership changes
forecasts.update_triggers: Labour leadership challenge; major Reform scandal; significant economic improvement; snap election call

forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2035?
forecasts.key_change: Huang AGI claim sparks debate but no consensus; capability gains continue on trajectory
forecasts.base_rate: Median expert estimate for AGI clusters around 2030-2040; Metaculus community median ~2032

factors_up.factor: Rapid capability scaling: Claude solving 5-hour engineering tasks at 50% reliability
Massive investment acceleration: OpenAI $122B round at $852B valuation
Agentic AI making rapid progress in real-world task completion

factors_down.factor: Current systems still fail at novel physical reasoning and sustained strategy
Shift from hype to evaluation suggests diminishing marginal returns
Definitional ambiguity means even claimed AGI may not meet resolution criteria

forecasts.key_uncertainties: Whether current scaling paradigm hits fundamental limits; definition alignment; geopolitical disruptions to AI development
forecasts.update_triggers: Major capability plateau; breakthrough in reasoning/generalization; regulatory intervention slowing development

forecasts.question: Will China initiate a military blockade or invasion of Taiwan before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.key_change: PLA drone flew through Taiwan's Pratas Island airspace (Jan 17) — possible first territorial airspace violation in decades
forecasts.base_rate: No Chinese military invasion of a sovereign territory since 1979 (Sino-Vietnamese War); base rate for major power invasion ~1-2% per year

factors_up.factor: PLA drone violated Taiwan territorial airspace over Pratas Island (Jan 17, 2026)
100+ aircraft, 90 crossing median line in single day (Dec 29, 2025)
Chinese policy community increasingly convinced action will happen; perception Trump won't defend Taiwan
Foreign Affairs: 'Perfect Storm for Taiwan in 2026'

factors_down.factor: China's economy faces significant headwinds; invasion would devastate trade
US military presence in Indo-Pacific remains strong deterrent
Pattern remains coercion and gray-zone tactics, not kinetic escalation

forecasts.key_uncertainties: Whether Pratas airspace violation signals new phase or is one-off provocation; Trump administration response posture; internal CCP decision-making dynamics
forecasts.update_triggers: PLA exercises simulating blockade; US-China diplomatic breakdown; Taiwan independence declaration; direct military confrontation

forecasts.question: Will UK annual CPI inflation fall below 2.0% for at least one month before 1 January 2027?
forecasts.key_change: CPI at 3.0% in Feb; BoE expects ~3.5% in March due to Middle East conflict energy price impacts
forecasts.base_rate: UK CPI was below 2% for only 4 months in past 5 years (Sep-Dec 2024)

factors_up.factor: Only needs one month below 2.0% in remaining 9 months (Apr-Dec 2026)
Potential for energy price reversal if Middle East tensions ease

factors_down.factor: CPI at 3.0% in Feb, expected to rise to 3.5% in March — moving away from target
Middle East conflict driving energy and commodity price increases
Core CPI at 3.2% and rising; broad-based inflationary pressures
BoE held rates at 3.75% in March, signaling inflation concern

forecasts.key_uncertainties: Duration and intensity of Middle East energy shock; wage growth trajectory; imported inflation from global tariff changes
forecasts.update_triggers: Sudden energy price collapse; major recession; March CPI data (Apr 22)

forecasts.question: Will Spider-Man: Brand New Day gross over $1.5B worldwide by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.key_change: Trailer hits 1B views — first ever; projected $2.54B worldwide; release July 31
forecasts.base_rate: Spider-Man: No Way Home grossed $1.9B; 4 of 8 Spider-Man films crossed $1B

factors_up.factor: Trailer broke all records with 1 billion views in under a week
Industry projection of $2.54B worldwide — well above $1.5B threshold
Expected biggest opening since Avengers: Endgame
MCU Phase 6 momentum; strong franchise brand

factors_down.factor: Release July 31 — only 5 months to reach $1.5B by Dec 31 (still ample)
Avengers: Doomsday competition in same year could split audience
Possible audience fatigue with superhero content

forecasts.key_uncertainties: Review reception; competition from other summer blockbusters; global economic conditions affecting discretionary spending
forecasts.update_triggers: Critical reviews; opening weekend performance; international market response

forecasts.question: Will OpenAI and Anthropic have a combined ARR exceeding $70B by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.key_change: Combined current ARR ~$44B (OpenAI ~$25B + Anthropic ~$19B); Anthropic growth accelerating but $70B needs strong H2
forecasts.base_rate: No SaaS companies have grown this fast; but AI revenue growth is unprecedented in enterprise software

factors_up.factor: Anthropic projected to reach $30B ARR by end 2026 (Coatue estimate)
OpenAI projected $29.4B full-year 2026 revenue; ARR likely higher by Q4
Enterprise AI adoption accelerating; Claude Code driving Anthropic growth
Both companies showing sustained >2x annual growth

factors_down.factor: Current combined ~$44B needs ~60% growth in 9 months to reach $70B
Revenue accounting differences complicate comparison (gross vs net)
Growth rates projected to slow in H2 2026
Macro headwinds and tariff uncertainty could slow enterprise spending

forecasts.key_uncertainties: Whether Anthropic's 10x annual growth rate sustains; OpenAI's monetization trajectory; definition of ARR vs revenue
forecasts.update_triggers: Q2 revenue reports; major enterprise contract announcements; competitive dynamics with Google/Meta

forecasts.question: Will UK average annual real GDP growth exceed 1.5% across the 2030s (2030-2039)?
forecasts.key_change: OBR March 2026 forecast averages 1.6% medium-term; but OECD cut 2026 forecast to 0.7%
forecasts.base_rate: UK averaged ~1.4% real GDP growth 2010-2019; 2000-2009 averaged ~1.8%

factors_up.factor: OBR medium-term forecast averages 1.6% — just above threshold
AI productivity gains could boost growth in 2030s
Post-Brexit adjustment period ending; potential trade deals

factors_down.factor: OECD cut 2026 forecast to 0.7%; Middle East conflict dragging growth
UK GDP per capita declining; structural productivity challenges worsening
Aging population and fiscal constraints limit growth potential

forecasts.key_uncertainties: AI productivity impact timing; demographic trends; trade policy evolution; energy transition costs vs benefits
forecasts.update_triggers: Major structural reform; AI productivity breakthrough evidence; sustained OBR forecast revisions; demographic policy changes
